mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly drive corporate earnings growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in more than 65% of expansion-phase observations, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 200, elevated inflation expectations force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of periods where the CPI index exceeds 200, or the signal fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of recessionary observations, or the 2-month decline threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.
